en.extract:
The crescent-chested babbler (Cyanoderma melanothorax) is a babbler species in the family Timaliidae and is native to the Indonesian islands of Java and Bali. It inhabits subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest, montane forest and shrubland. It is listed as Least Concern on the IUCN Red List.
It is ochreous-brown, has rufous-coloured wings, paler underparts and a black crescent across its breast. It is 13 cm (5.1 in) long.
Myiothera melanothorax was the scientific name proposed by Coenraad Jacob Temminck in 1823 who described a babbler from Java.
The generic name Cyanoderma was proposed by Tommaso Salvadori in 1874 for babblers with slender and pointed beaks.
It was later placed in the genus Stachyris, but since 2020 is recognised as a Cyanoderma species.


References


External links
BirdLife International (2019). "Crescent-chested Babbler Cyanoderma melanothorax".

The '''crescent-chested babbler''' ('''''Cyanoderma melanothorax''''') is a babbler species in the family [[Timaliidae]] and is native to the [[Indonesia]]n islands of [[Java]] and [[Bali]]. It inhabits [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]], [[montane forest]] and [[shrubland]]. It is listed as [[Least Concern]] on the [[IUCN Red List]].<ref name="iucn status 16 November 2021" />

It is ochreous-brown, has rufous-coloured wings, paler underparts and a black crescent across its breast. It is {{convert|13|cm|abbr=on}} long.<ref name=HBoW2016>{{cite book |year=2020 |editor=del Hoyo, J. |editor2=Elliott, A. |editor3=Sargatal, J. |editor4=Christie, D. A. |editor5=de Juana, E. |title=Handbook of the Birds of the World |publisher=Lynx Edicions |location=Barcelona |volume=2: Passerines |last1=Collar |first1=N. J. |last2=Robson |first2=C. |chapter=Crescent-chested Babbler (''Cyanoderma melanothorax'') |doi=10.2173/bow.crcbab1.01 |s2cid=243136418 |chapter-url=https://www.hbw.com/species/crescent-chested-babbler-cyanoderma-melanothorax }}</ref>

''Myiothera melanothorax'' was the scientific name proposed by [[Coenraad Jacob Temminck]] in 1823 who [[scientific description|described]] a babbler from Java.<ref>{{cite book |last1=Temminck |first1=C. J. |year=1823 |title=Nouveau recueil de planches coloriées d'oiseaux: pour servir de suite et de complément aux planches enluminées de Buffon, édition in-folio et in-4⁰ de l'Imprimerie royale, 1770 |publisher=F. G. Levrault |location=Strasbourg |chapter=Myiothera melanothorax |page=first page |chapter-url=https://archive.org/stream/Nouveaurecueild2Temm#page/262/mode/2up}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |last1=Temminck |first1=C. J. |year=1823 |title=Nouveau recueil de planches coloriées d'oiseaux: pour servir de suite et de complément aux planches enluminées de Buffon, édition in-folio et in-4⁰ de l'Imprimerie royale, 1770  |publisher=F. G. Levrault |location=Strasbourg |chapter=Myiothera melanothorax |page=second page |chapter-url=https://archive.org/details/Nouveaurecueild5Temm/page/n381}}</ref>
The generic name ''Cyanoderma'' was proposed by [[Tommaso Salvadori]] in 1874 for babblers with slender and pointed beaks.<ref>{{cite book |last1=Salvadori |first1=T. |year=1874 |title=Catalogo sistematico degli uccelli di Borneo |volume=V |location=Genova |publisher=Museo Civico di Storia Naturale |chapter=Gen. nov. Cyanoderma, Salvad. |pages=213–215 |chapter-url=https://archive.org/details/catalogosistemat00salv/page/212}}</ref>
It was later placed in the genus ''[[Stachyris]]'', but since 2020 is recognised as a ''[[Cyanoderma]]'' species.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Moyle |first1=R. G. |last2=Andersen |first2=M. J. |last3=Oliveros |first3=C. H. |last4=Steinheimer |first4=F. D. |last5=Reddy |first5=S. |year=2012 |title=Phylogeny and Biogeography of the Core Babblers (Aves: Timaliidae) |journal=Systematic Biology |volume=61 |issue=4 |pages=631–651 |doi=10.1093/sysbio/sys027 |pmid=22328569|doi-access=free }}</ref><ref name=HBoW2016/>

zh.extract:
珠颊穗鹛（学名：Stachyris melanothorax），是画眉科穗鹛属的一种，为印度尼西亚的特有种。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
珠颊穗鹛的栖息地包括亚热带或热带的湿润低地林、亚热带或热带的（低地）湿润疏灌丛和亚热带或热带的湿润山地林。


参考文献
